ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region the country belongs to */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code for the country */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographical latitude of the country */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographical longitude of the country */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the country data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the country data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clusters" (
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cluster */,
  "cluster_name" TEXT /* Name of the cluster */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the cluster */,
  "cluster_type" TEXT /* Type of cluster (e.g., geographic, demographic, etc.) */,
  "description_long" TEXT /* Detailed description of the cluster */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cluster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "epidemic_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country */,
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* ID of the cluster */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the data */,
  "new_cases_per_million" REAL /* New cases per million population */,
  "icu_patients_per_million" REAL /* ICU patients per million population */,
  "hosp_patients_per_million" REAL /* Hospital patients per million population */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of epidemic data (e.g., covid, influenza, etc.) */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_epidemic_data_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_epidemic_data_cluster_id FOREIGN KEY ("cluster_id") REFERENCES clusters ("cluster_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_epidemic_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic data point */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Age group (e.g., 0-19, 20-39, etc.) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the age group */,
  "age_group_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the age group */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender distribution for the age group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "healthcare_capacity" (
  "capacity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each healthcare capacity data point */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country */,
  "icu_beds" INTEGER /* Number of ICU beds */,
  "hospital_beds" INTEGER /* Number of hospital beds */,
  "staff_capacity" INTEGER /* Number of healthcare staff available */,
  "equipment_capacity" INTEGER /* Number of medical equipment available */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("capacity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_healthcare_capacity_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, etc.) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "email_verified" INTEGER /* Whether the user's email address has been verified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit, etc.) */,
  "action_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the action performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country */,
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* ID of the cluster */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., outbreak, unusual pattern, etc.) */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  "alert_details" TEXT /* Detailed description of the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_cluster_id FOREIGN KEY ("cluster_id") REFERENCES clusters ("cluster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date of the notification */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., alert, update, etc.) */,
  "notification_read" INTEGER /* Whether the notification has been read by the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_quality" (
  "quality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data quality metric */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point */,
  "quality_score" REAL /* Quality score of the data point */,
  "quality_criteria" TEXT /* Criteria used to evaluate data quality */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("quality_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_quality_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES epidemic_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  "data_format" TEXT /* Format of the data (e.g., CSV, JSON, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_validation" (
  "validation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data validation rule */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point */,
  "validation_rule" TEXT /* Validation rule for the data point */,
  "validation_status" TEXT /* Status of the validation (e.g., passed, failed, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("validation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_validation_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES epidemic_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., summary, detailed, etc.) */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "visualizations" (
  "visualization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each visualization */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report the visualization belongs to */,
  "visualization_type" TEXT /* Type of visualization (e.g., chart, graph, etc.) */,
  "visualization_data" TEXT /* Data used to generate the visualization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("visualization_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_visualizations_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "machine_learning_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of model (e.g., regression, classification, etc.) */,
  "model_version" TEXT /* Version of the model */,
  "model_parameters" TEXT /* Hyperparameters used to train the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model that made the prediction */,
  "prediction_date" TEXT /* Date the prediction was made */,
  "predicted_value" REAL /* Predicted value */,
  "prediction_uncertainty" REAL /* Uncertainty of the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES machine_learning_models ("model_id")
)
